title: Generative artificial intelligence for construction: Use cases, trends, challenges, and opportunities
contribution: 通过混合方法综述（文献计量分析+定性系统综述），系统梳理了生成式AI在建筑行业中的五大应用领域、六大挑战和四级未来研究路线图
abstract: This paper conducts a mixed-method review combining bibliometric analysis with qualitative thematic synthesis to examine generative AI applications in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) industry. A total of 148 publications from Scopus and Google Scholar (2014-2024) were analyzed. The bibliometric analysis identified 49 high-frequency keywords grouped into six thematic clusters. The qualitative synthesis examined five dominant application domains: (1) proactive safety monitoring and risk prevention, (2) generative AI for sustainable construction, (3) automating design through generative intelligence, (4) construction education, and (5) construction management. Six recurring challenges were identified: data availability and quality, computational complexity and costs, integration with existing workflows, ethical and regulatory concerns, workforce skill gaps, and reliability and trust. The study proposes a four-level research roadmap spanning industry-level considerations, organizational and stakeholder perspectives, project-level perspectives, and technological integration.
methodology: Mixed-method review combining quantitative bibliometric analysis with qualitative systematic synthesis. 148 publications were retrieved from Scopus and Google Scholar (2014-2024), filtered using PRISMA criteria. VOSviewer was used for keyword co-occurrence network mapping and citation analysis, identifying 6 bibliometric clusters from 49 high-frequency keywords. Full-text qualitative review reclassified articles into 5 overarching thematic areas through inductive coding, followed by development of a forward-looking four-level research roadmap.
results: Publication output grew rapidly from 1 paper in 2018 to 82 by September 2024. The largest thematic area was automating design through generative intelligence (33% of papers), followed by proactive safety monitoring (23%), construction management (20%), construction education (13%), and sustainable construction (11%). Six keyword clusters were identified: construction safety and monitoring, BIM and generative design, architectural and structural design, LLM in the AEC industry, AI in education, and GPT-driven automation in project management. The study identified six major adoption challenges and proposed a structured research roadmap across industry, organizational, project, and technological levels.
